#libs
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```


# metadata
```{r}
ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_dups_samples_batch_check_all_physeq_wgs.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_RESEQUENCING_wgs.rds')
#ps@sam_data$Age = as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Age)
#ps@sam_data$Weight <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Weight)
#ps@sam_data$Height <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Height)
#saveRDS(ps, '/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
#meta <- read_csv('metadata_eng_prepared.csv')

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}
# Get categorical variables (already determined)
# Remove 'timepoint' and 'Group' (assuming 'Group' is your group variable name)

meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

# Check the structure of Timepoint column
cat("Class of Timepoint column:", class(meta$Timepoint), "\n")
cat("Unique values in Timepoint:", unique(meta$Timepoint), "\n")

meta <- meta %>% 
  filter(Timepoint == '-1')

cat("\nNumber of samples in Timepoint == '-1':", nrow(meta), "\n")

# Using the entire meta dataframe (no timepoint filtering)
categorical_vars <- names(meta)[sapply(meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

# Remove 'timepoint' from the list if it exists
# Remove 'Group' from categorical_vars since that's our grouping variable
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age',"Group", 'Height', 'No_Patient','Weight','Timepoint','SampleID'))

# Check what we have
cat("Group variable: Group\n")
cat("Number of groups:", length(unique(meta$Group)), "\n")
cat("Categorical variables to plot:", length(categorical_vars), "\n")
print(categorical_vars)
cat("\nTotal observations in dataset:", nrow(meta), "\n")

# Select only the columns we need
df_f <- meta %>%
  select(Group, all_of(categorical_vars))

# Create long format while preserving Group
df_long <- df_f %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Group,
               names_to = "Column_Name",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  # Remove rows with NA values in Value
  filter(!is.na(Value))

# Calculate percentages WITHIN each Value (category level)
# This means for each categorical variable value, what's the distribution of Groups?
df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Column_Name, Value, Group) %>%
  tally() %>%
  group_by(Column_Name, Value) %>%  # Group by Column_Name and Value for percentages
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percent = n/total * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plotting parameters - adjust as needed
size <- 15  # Base text size

# Create the plot: 100% stacked bars with Group as fill, x = Value
p <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = Value, y = percent, fill = Group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = size/2, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ Column_Name, scales = "free", ncol = 7) +
  labs(title = "Percentage Distribution by Group for Each Categorical Variable",
       #subtitle = paste("Total N =", nrow(meta)),
       x = "Category Values",
       y = "Percentage",
       fill = "Group") +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = size+15, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = size+15, color = "black", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(size = size + 15, color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = size+35),
    legend.title = element_text(size = size + 35),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.2),
    strip.text = element_text(size = size+10, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray80", color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = size + 35, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = size+35)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("A" = "#2c7bb6", "B" = "#d7191c", "C" = "#1a9641")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)))

# Display the plot
print(p)

# Save to PDF
if (length(categorical_vars) > 0) {
  pdf_width <- min(45, 9 + 7 * ceiling(length(categorical_vars)/4))
  pdf_height <- min(70, 6 * ceiling(length(categorical_vars)/4))
  
  pdf("metadata_percentage_by_group_stacked_v3.pdf", width = pdf_width, height = pdf_height)
  print(p)
  dev.off()
  
  cat("\nPDF file saved: metadata_percentage_by_group_stacked.pdf\n")
} else {
  cat("\nNo categorical variables to plot!\n")
}
```


```
